content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 13 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 106r–107v, LC PDF pages 210–211 | **Source Images:** DEU_013_page_001.jpg, DEU_013_page_002.jpg | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pa_0152 — DEU_013_vaticanus_page_001.jpg

---

**1** All the word that I am commanding you, that you shall keep to do; you shall not add to it and you shall not diminish from it.

**2** If there arises in your midst a prophet or a dreamer of a dream, and he gives you a sign or a wonder,

**3** and the sign or the wonder comes about which he spoke to you, saying, "Let us go after other gods"—which you have not known—"and let us serve them,"

**4** you shall not listen to the words of that prophet or to that dreamer of a dream, for the LORD your God is testing you, to know whether you love the LORD your God with all your heart and with all your being.

**5** After the LORD your God you shall walk, and him you shall fear, and his commandments you shall keep, and to his voice you shall listen, and him you shall serve, and to him you shall cling.

**6** And that prophet or that dreamer of a dream shall be put to death, for he has spoken apostasy against the LORD your God—who brought you out from the land of Egypt and redeemed you from the house of slaves—to thrust you from the way in which the LORD your God commanded you to walk; and you shall burn out the evil from your midst.

**7** If your brother, the son of your mother, or your son or your daughter or the wife of your bosom or your companion who is as your own being, should entice you secretly, saying, "Let us go and serve other gods"—which you have not known, you nor your fathers—

**8** from the gods of the peoples who are around you, those near to you or those far from you, from the one end of the land to the other end of the land—

**9** you shall not consent to him and you shall not listen to him, and your eye shall not take pity on him, and you shall not spare [him], and you shall not cover [for] him.

**10** But you shall surely kill him; your hand shall be upon him first to put him to death, and the hand of all the people afterward.

**11** And you shall stone him with stones, and he shall die, because he sought to thrust you away from the LORD your God, who brought you out from the land of Egypt, from the house of slaves.

**12** And all Yisra'el shall hear and shall fear, and shall not continue to do a thing like this evil in your midst.

**13** If you hear, in one of your cities which the LORD your God is giving you to dwell there, saying:

**14** "Wicked men have gone out from your midst and have driven away the inhabitants of their city, saying, 'Let us go and serve other gods,' which you have not known"—

**15** then you shall inquire and search and ask thoroughly; and behold, if the thing is established [as] true—this abomination was done in your midst—

**16** you shall surely strike the inhabitants of that city with the edge of the sword; devote it to destruction [as] cherem, and all that is in it and its livestock, with the edge of the sword.

**17** And all its spoil you shall gather into the midst of its open square, and you shall burn with fire the city and all its spoil, all of it, to the LORD your God; and it shall be a mound forever; it shall not be built again.

**18** And nothing of the devoted thing [cherem] shall cling to your hand, so that the LORD may turn from the fierceness of his anger and give you compassion and be compassionate to you and multiply you, as he swore to your fathers—

**19** when you listen to the voice of the LORD your God, to keep all his commandments which I am commanding you today, to do what is right in the eyes of the LORD your God.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

**On verse numbering:** The MT/LC numbering begins this chapter at v.1 with the summary verse ("All the word that I command you..."), which many English translations place as the final verse of ch. 12 (12:32). The LC is followed here; verse count = 19.

**v.1 — shmar la'asot:** Literally "keep to do," a characteristic Deuteronomic idiom pairing the verbs. The LC text clearly reads lo tosiph and lo tigra'—"you shall not add" and "you shall not diminish." LXX Vaticanus (p. 152) reads οὐ προσθήσεις... οὐκ ἀφελεῖς, agreeing exactly.

**v.2 — navi o cholem chalom:** "A prophet or a dreamer of a dream." The doubled noun construction is literal. LXX has προφήτης ἢ ἐνυπνιαζόμενος ἐνύπνιον. The LC marks the parasha boundary here (setumah marker visible in the manuscript).

**v.4 — massah:** "Testing you" (בְּחַן). The LORD is said to be testing (bachan) the people's loyalty — a key Deuteronomic theology of divine proving. LXX πειράζει.

**v.6 — hu dibber sarah 'al YHWH:** "He has spoken apostasy against the LORD." Sarah is a rare noun (apostasy/turning aside). LXX has ἀπέστησεν ("led away").

**v.7 — 'eshet cheqekha:** "The wife of your bosom" — a vivid idiom for closest conjugal intimacy. LXX γυναῖκα τὴν ἐν κόλπῳ σου. The list of enticers (brother/son/daughter/wife/companion) is one of the most intimate catalogs in Torah law.

**v.9 — lo tachos 'einekha:** "Your eye shall not take pity" — three negative commands in sequence: do not consent, do not listen, do not pity. The LC reads lo tachmol and lo tekasseh, a triad of restraint against natural affection.

**v.10 — yadekha tihyeh vo ba-ri'shonah:** "Your hand shall be upon him first" — the witness is to cast the first stone. This prevents false accusation; the accuser must bear the first cost.

**v.13 — ir ha-niddachat:** "A city led astray" (עִיר הַנִּדַּחַת). The technical term for the apostate city whose entire population has been seduced into idolatry. LC clearly shows the Niphal participle niddachat. LXX has πόλιν... ἀποστάσαν.

**v.16 — herem:** "Devoted to destruction." The root חרם (cherem) denotes total consecration/destruction — nothing may be retained. LC reads tachariym otah cherem, a cognate absolute construction.

**v.17 — tel 'olam:** "A mound forever" — the site is to remain a permanent ruin, a visible monument to divine judgment. LXX χῶμα ἀοίκητον — an uninhabitable mound.

**LXX comparison (Vaticanus p. 152):** The Greek text on this page is closely aligned with MT throughout ch. 13. One notable variation: the LXX lacks the phrase "to your eyes" (le'einekha) in v.18's closing clause, a minor minus not affecting meaning. The LXX adds εἰς πάντα throughout as a rhetorical intensifier in several verses where the MT is brief.

— *EMET*
